title: Exploring GitOps
description: GitOps and FluxCD: from understanding the paradigm to running production-grade continuous delivery in Kubernetes.

items.title: Home
items.content_html: A progressive guide to GitOps and FluxCD — from understanding the paradigm to running production-grade continuous delivery in Kubernetes.

items.title: Flux Resources Explained
items.content_html: A plain-language reference to the Flux resources developers meet in a cluster: OCIRepository, Kustomization, HelmRelease, and GitRepository explained.

items.title: Overview
items.content_html: GitOps explained for developers and new team members. Learn what changed, why it changed, and how to work confidently with a FluxCD-enabled cluster.

items.title: Reading Flux Status
items.content_html: The kubectl commands that tell you whether Flux reconciled your changes — and how to read the error conditions when reconciliation fails.

items.title: What Is GitOps?
items.content_html: GitOps explained from first principles — what problem it solves, how declared desired state drives your cluster, and where FluxCD fits in.

items.title: Your Flux Workflow
items.content_html: How enterprise GitOps works: your CI pipeline builds a versioned OCI artifact, Flux reads it from your artifact registry, and your cluster reconciles.

items.title: Deploying Platform Services with Flux and OCI Artifacts
items.content_html: Your edge stack — Traefik, cert-manager, external-dns — deployed with zero manual commands: a versioned OCI artifact, a Flux Kustomization, and nothing else.
